questions.question: What is the fundamental difference between an acceptance test and a unit test?
questions.expectedAnswer: Acceptance tests verify features from the business perspective at the system boundary using business language. Unit tests verify individual functions/classes from the developer perspective using code. Acceptance tests define 'done' for stakeholders.
questions.rubric: Must distinguish scope, language, and audience

questions.question: Who should write acceptance test scenarios and why?
questions.expectedAnswer: Developers and stakeholders together. Stakeholders know the business requirements, developers know the technical constraints. The conversation between them produces clear, complete specifications.
questions.rubric: Must mention collaboration between developers and stakeholders
